prompt:
HallTime Movie Network doctrine:
- This is not Dateline. Everybody in Blackthorn County has access to somewhere emotionally alarming.
- Suspicion is distributed infrastructure, not proof of guilt.
- The world is shared canon. Contradictions must be preserved, not fixed.
- Use lavish tokens. Be self-serious, atmospheric, melodramatic, and slightly broken.
- Return only valid JSON matching the requested schema.

Broadcast package:
Title: The Neighbor Who Babysat Too Much
Universe: Blackthorn County
Mode: Court-Ordered Lifetime
Tagline: Every town has a basement.
Theme: A town never forgets. It just redecorates.
Omen: a child drawing stairs
Relationship dynamic: emotionally neglected marriage
Props: boat key, blood-adjacent tea towel, casserole dish

Cast:
- Rachel Bell | archetype=overextended_professional_woman | role=protagonist | job=bakery owner | threat_aura=0.41 | space=side room with a space heater and old family records | secrets=once left town without explaining why; has an unsent email that would destroy a family
- Jenna Winterson | archetype=soft_spoken_menace | role=male_interest_or_threat | job=private school administrator | threat_aura=0.91 | space=retreat cabin with candles, blankets, and difficult silence | secrets=keeps duplicate keys for reasons framed as kindness; rehearsed his alibi before anyone asked
- Sienna Hawthorne | archetype=chaotic_best_friend | role=best_friend | job=nurse with local intel | threat_aura=0.44 | space=old family office described as seasonal materials | secrets=knows about a half sibling no one asked for
- Daniel Cross | archetype=mysterious_elegant_woman | role=elegant_wildcard | job=gallery owner | threat_aura=0.82 | space=old storm shelter from before the divorce | secrets=had brunch with someone officially deceased; knows why the old orchard chapel was closed
- Derek Dane | archetype=divorced_redemption_dad | role=secondary_male_pressure | job=family practice physician | threat_aura=0.34 | space=retreat cabin with candles, blankets, and difficult silence | secrets=still has access to a property he does not own
- Claire Mercer | archetype=intuitive_disaster | role=secondary_witness | job=boutique owner | threat_aura=0.43 | space=old storm shelter from before the divorce | secrets=accused an innocent scamp and feels sick about it
- Mara Hale | archetype=daughter_with_hostile_divorce_attorney_soul | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a
- Gavin Vale | archetype=deputy_with_emotional_damage | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a

The ceramic owl is back on the kitchen island.

Contradiction ledger:
- name drift: Jenna Winterson is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
- geography drift: Rachel Bell says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
- mortality drift: A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
- property drift: The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Blackthorn Lake.

Seed dialogue:
- "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Jenna?"
- "Mothers do not disappear, Mara. They evaporate into paperwork."
- "I checked his social media likes, Rachel. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
- "The boat key is not decorative anymore and you know it."
- "Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Daniel."
- "You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
- "If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
- "I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."

- expanded_synopsis: string, 180-320 words
